---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

<center>
[<i class="fa fa-file-pdf"></i> Download PDF](content/cv_lemus-canovas.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"}
</center>

<br>

## Current Position

**Beatriu de Pinós Postdoctoral Fellow** · *since February 2026*  
Barcelona Supercomputing Center — Earth Sciences Department, Spain

---

## Previous Positions

| Period | Position | Institution |
|---|---|---|
| Oct 2024 – Feb 2026 | Seal of Excellence Postdoctoral Fellow | EURAC Research, Center for Climate Change and Transformation, Italy |
| Oct 2023 – Sep 2024 | Postdoctoral Researcher (Xunta de Galicia) + Visiting Postdoc | USC / EURAC Research, Italy |
| Oct 2022 – Sep 2023 | Postdoctoral Researcher (Xunta de Galicia) + Visiting Postdoc | USC / Andorra Research+Innovation |
| May 2022 – Aug 2022 | Postdoctoral Researcher | Andorra Research+Innovation |
| Nov 2021 – Feb 2022 | Adjunct Professor | University of Barcelona, Dept. of Geography |
| Oct 2018 – Oct 2021 | Predoctoral Researcher FPU | University of Barcelona, Dept. of Geography |
| Apr 2018 – Sep 2018 | Predoctoral Researcher APIF | University of Barcelona, Dept. of Geography |
| Oct 2017 – Nov 2017 | JAE-Intro Fellow | Spanish National Research Council, Institute of Geosciences |

---

## Education

**PhD in Geography** · University of Barcelona, Spain · 2022  
**MSc in Remote Sensing and GIS** · Autonomous University of Barcelona, Spain · 2017  
**Graduated in Geography** · University of Barcelona, Spain · 2016

---

## Research Projects

**As Principal Investigator:**

1. **HIPEAtMount** — Tailored attribution of high-impact precipitation events to anthropogenic climate change in mountain areas. Province of Bolzano/Bozen, Italy. 2024–2026. **€172,750**
2. **ATOC** — Heat wave attribution to climate change in Barcelona. Barcelona City Council. 2023–2024. **€24,500**

**As Collaborator:**

3. **EO4Multihazards** — Earth Observation for Multi-Hazard Risk Assessments. European Space Agency. PI: Terzi, S. 2023–2025. €1M (share €144,000 to EURAC)
4. **FutureMed** — Transdisciplinary network bridging climate science and societal impacts. COST. PI: Khodayar, S. 2023–2027
5. **EXE** — Compound dry and warm events in mainland Spain. Spanish Ministry of Science and Innovation. PI: Martin-Vide, J. 2021–2024. €66,500
6. **CLICES** — Climate of the last century in Spanish mainland. Spanish Ministry of Science and Innovation. PI: Martin-Vide, J. & Lopez-Bustins, J.A. 2018–2020. €78,000

---

## Awards & Distinctions

- **Beatriu de Pinós Fellowship** (2026) — Generalitat de Catalunya / Barcelona Supercomputing Center
- **Seal of Excellence**, Marie Skłodowska-Curie European Fellowship call
- **HIPEAtMount grant** (2024) — Province of Bolzano, Italy (€172,750)
- **International Eduard Fontserè Meteorology Prize** (€2,500)
- **Antoni M. Badia i Margarit Prize** (€1,600)
- **Extraordinary Doctorate Prize** — University of Barcelona, 2022
- **Best Poster Award** — 7th International Conference on Meteorology and Climatology of the Mediterranean (MetMed), 2019
- **FPU Predoctoral Fellowship** (FPU2017/02166) — Spanish Ministry of Education, 2018–2022

---

## Selected Publications

*H-index: 14 (Scopus), 15 (Google Scholar) · Citations: 500 (Scopus), 677 (Google Scholar) · 29 publications, 13 as first author*

1. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Crespi, A., Maines, E., Terzi, S., and Pittore, M. (2025). More intense heatwaves under drier conditions: a compound event analysis in the Adige River basin. *Hydrology and Earth System Sciences*, 29, 6781–6809. (D1, IF: 5.9) · [doi](https://doi.org/10.5194/hess-29-6781-2025)

2. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Gonzalez-Herrero, S., Trapero, L., et al. (2025). Exploring the interplay between observed warming, atmospheric circulation, and soil–atmosphere feedbacks on heatwaves in a temperate mountain region. *Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences*, 25, 2503–2518. (Q1, IF: 4.7) · [doi](https://doi.org/10.5194/nhess-25-2503-2025)

3. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Insua-Costa, D., Trigo, R. M., & Miralles, D. G. (2024). Record-shattering 2023 Spring heatwave in western Mediterranean amplified by long-term drought. *npj Climate and Atmospheric Science*, 7, 1–8. (D1, IF: 8.5) · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41612-024-00569-6)

4. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Montesinos-Ciuró, E., Cearreta-Innocenti, T., Serrano-Notivoli, R., & Royé, D. (2024). Attribution of the unprecedented heat event of August 2023 in Barcelona to observed and projected global warming. *Urban Climate*, 56, 102019. (Q1, IF: 6.0) · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.uclim.2024.102019)

5. Serrano-Notivoli, R., **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, et al. (2022). Heat and cold waves in mainland Spain: Origins, characteristics, and trends. *Weather and Climate Extremes*, 37, 100471. (D1, IF: 7.8) · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.wace.2022.100471)

6. **Lemus-Canovas, M.** (2022). Changes in compound monthly precipitation and temperature extremes and their relationship with teleconnection patterns in the Mediterranean. *Journal of Hydrology*, 608, 127580. (D1, IF: 6.7) · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jhydrol.2022.127580)

7. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, & Lopez-Bustins, J. A. (2021). Assessing internal changes in the future structure of dry–hot compound events: the case of the Pyrenees. *Natural Hazards and Earth System Sciences*, 21(6), 1721–1738. · [doi](https://doi.org/10.5194/nhess-21-1721-2021)

8. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Martin-Vide, J., Moreno-Garcia, M. C., & Lopez-Bustins, J. A. (2020). Estimating Barcelona's metropolitan daytime hot and cold poles using Landsat-8 LST. *Science of The Total Environment*, 699, 134307. · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.scitotenv.2019.134307)

9. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Lopez-Bustins, J. A., Trapero, L., & Martin-Vide, J. (2019). Combining circulation weather types and daily precipitation modelling to derive climatic precipitation regions in the Pyrenees. *Atmospheric Research*, 220, 181–193. · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.atmosres.2019.01.018)

10. **Lemus-Canovas, M.**, Lopez-Bustins, J. A., Martin-Vide, J., & Royé, D. (2019). synoptReg: An R package for computing a synoptic climate classification and a spatial regionalization of environmental data. *Environmental Modelling & Software*, 118, 114–119. · [doi](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.envsoft.2019.04.006)

See the full publication list on the [Research](research.qmd) page or [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=iOCaO7sAAAAJ).

---

## Teaching & Supervision

- **270 hours** of university teaching at the University of Barcelona; 42 hours in master's programs
- **3 courses** on spatial data analysis with R, Faculty of Geography and History, UB (2019–2021)
- **Supervision:** 1 Bachelor thesis, 2 Master theses (UB, TU Dresden); support to 1 PhD student (UniBz–EURAC)
- **Jury member**, doctoral thesis, Universidad San Jorge (Zaragoza), 2023
- **Reviewer**, Sixth IPCC Assessment Report

---

## Skills

| Category | Tools |
|---|---|
| Programming | R, Python |
| Climate data | NetCDF, ERA5, EURO-CORDEX, STEAD |
| R packages | tidyverse, metR, synoptReg, ggplot2, sf |
| Version control | Git, GitHub |
| Writing | Quarto, RMarkdown, LaTeX |

---

`r source("_common.R"); last_updated()`
